comment: Evaluator-binding contract for north-star decode-attention candidates. A binding template specifies EXACTLY what a candidate must declare + produce to become an executable decode_eval candidate, and what is still missing. It builds no kernel. The north-star template is concrete_runner_status=deferred_no_kernel (well-specified, blocked only on a kernel + runner). The selftest template is executable plumbing (no performance claim).

templates.comparator: gqa_coop_vec (current winning decode attention) / canonical decode route — NEVER a weaker baseline
templates.local_ab_runner: extra/qk_north_star_flash_attn_tile_ab.py [IMPLEMENTED]: warp-cooperative q.k partial (ds_bpermute, LDS K/V, GQA pack, register online softmax) + many-workgroup combine, vs gqa_coop_vec, clock-pinned, byte-exact. MEASURED FAIL_LOCAL_AB 0.58x@ctx1024 / 0.89x@ctx4096.
templates.wd_runner: runtime_overhead with a flash_attn_tile decode route [NOT YET IMPLEMENTED]: requires a model route/flag that selects the tile in the decode attention path so extra/qk_decode_runtime_overhead.py measures whole-decode W==D; compares vs the canonical route W==D
templates.correctness_runner: byte-exact greedy vs gqa_coop_vec (preferred) OR teacher-forced dNLL <= 0.01

required_candidate_params.kv_split_count: split policy: MANY KV-splits, scaling with ctx; must be >= the comparator's split count (preserves T=1 parallel work)
required_candidate_params.flash_l: tile length / split granularity (e.g. the FLASH_L axis)
required_candidate_params.query_head_pack: GQA query-head column packing for coalesced fp16 loads (the gqa_coop_vec win)
required_candidate_params.gqa_grouping: Hq/Hkv grouping (Qwen3-8B: 32/8 = G4); query heads mapped to LOCAL threads
required_candidate_params.k_tile: K-tile batch length staged in LDS
required_candidate_params.v_tile: V-tile batch length staged in LDS
required_candidate_params.combine_strategy: efficient many-split / stream-k combine (NOT a naive serial reduce — the measured ceiling is the split-combine cost)
required_candidate_params.lds_bytes_per_workgroup: LDS K/V staging footprint per workgroup (must NOT collapse occupancy)
required_candidate_params.expected_workgroups_by_ctx: must GROW with ctx and be >= comparator (manufacture parallel work from KV splits)

gates.structural: expected_workgroups_by_ctx GROWS with ctx AND >= comparator (proves decode T=1 parallelism is preserved/enlarged); expected_no_wmma true; combine_kernel_count bounded (no per-output serial combine blowup)
gates.correctness: byte-exact greedy vs gqa_coop_vec OR dNLL <= 0.01
gates.local_ab: local flash_attn_tile attention us < comparator gqa_coop_vec us at ctx1024 (gate >= 1.05x); must beat the CURRENT WINNER, not a weak baseline
gates.wd: whole-decode W==D >= 5%@1024 or >= 7%@4096 vs the canonical route, no ctx512 regress > 1%, within the measured reproducibility band (<0.6% per the evaluator)
gates.promotion_policy: PASS_PROMOTE only PROPOSES an OWNER default decision; the loop never auto-promotes; q8/FLASH_L promotion rules unaffected

stop_conditions: collapses workgroups vs the comparator (violates the decode T=1 principle — fusion/LDS reuse that reduces workgroups is harmful)
benchmarks only a weak baseline instead of gqa_coop_vec / canonical route
local A/B <= comparator (does not beat the current winner)
W==D < 5%@1024 (and < 7%@4096) — local win does not transfer in-model
templates.local_ab_result: FAIL_LOCAL_AB: 0.58x@ctx1024, 0.89x@ctx4096 vs gqa_coop_vec (byte-exact, err 0.0). Attribution: the warp-cooperative q.k PARTIAL alone (~111us@1024) already exceeds coop's whole attention (~109us), and a many-workgroup combine (128 wg) is byte-for-byte as slow as the serial flash_reduce (32 wg) -> the combine is HBM-bandwidth-bound, not launch-bound. This REFUTES the prior 'serial-combine-is-the-ceiling' hypothesis; the real ceiling is the fused cooperative q.k partial vs coop's separated optimized matmul.
missing_for_executable: a whole-decode W==D route/flag (NOT pursued: the local A/B gate failed first, per the discipline 'if local A/B fails, stop before W==D route')
templates.notes: Kernel + local-A/B runner are now IMPLEMENTED and EXECUTABLE; the candidate is MEASURED and refuted at the local gate (0.58x@1024). A future candidate that beats coop locally would need a faster q.k partial (separated matmul or a better cooperative dot), not a better combine. See docs/north-star-flash-attn-tile-execution-result-20260621.md.

templates.comparator: n/a — selftest validates binding plumbing only; makes NO performance claim

gates.promotion_policy: never promotable; SELFTEST_PASS is not a performance pass

stop_conditions: must NOT be confused with north_star_flash_attn_tile_v0
must NOT report a performance verdict
templates.notes: Executable plumbing selftest: proves the binding -> generated candidate -> loop EXECUTE -> decode_eval -> SELFTEST_PASS artifact path works end-to-end, with NO benchmark and NO performance claim. Distinct from the deferred north-star candidate.

templates.comparator: gqa_coop_vec (current winner) — pure GPU time, apples-to-apples
templates.local_ab_runner: extra/qk_llama_flash_attn_tile_oracle_ab.py [IMPLEMENTED, PROFILING ORACLE]: llama flash_attn_tile+combine GPU time (rocprofv3 trace) vs coop GPU-busy (tinygrad ProfileGraphEvent). NOT a re-implemented kernel.
templates.correctness_runner: n/a — llama kernel is the REFERENCE (correct by construction); no re-implementation to verify

required_candidate_params.measurement: pure GPU kernel time both sides

gates.structural: n/a (oracle; measures the real llama kernel)
gates.local_ab: llama attention GPU us < coop GPU us @ctx1024, gate >= 1.05x llama-faster; MEASURED 5.71x
gates.wd: n/a (never routed; no W==D for a reference oracle)
gates.promotion_policy: NON-PROMOTABLE — vendored llama reference, not a tinygrad primitive; informs native codegen only; NEVER a default route

stop_conditions: must never become a default/model route
must never be claimed as a tinygrad primitive win
do not vendor llama machinery beyond the timing oracle
templates.local_ab_result: PASS_ORACLE_LOCAL_AB: llama 5.87/5.71/4.77x faster than gqa_coop_vec standalone @ctx512/1024/4096 (pure GPU: llama 10.2/12.2/27.7us vs coop 59.9/69.9/132us). The 10x decode-attention gap is a STANDALONE kernel-codegen target, NOT only in-model integration -> native codegen is aiming at the right layer; llama's kernel is the validated target + byte-level oracle for the codegen follow-up.
templates.notes: Reference oracle that resolved the central question (standalone kernel vs in-model integration): llama's flash_attn_tile beats coop STANDALONE ~5-6x. Full source port is BOUNDED (audited, ~700-900 lines, no cp_async/WMMA/broad-ggml) and deferred to the native-codegen follow-up that needs a re-runnable byte-level oracle. See docs/llama-flash-attn-tile-oracle-result-20260621.md.

templates.local_ab_runner: extra/qk_llama_flash_attn_tile_hcq_ab.py [IMPLEMENTED, HCQ CAPTURE-AND-REPLAY]: vendored llama flash_attn_tile<128,128,1,4,false>+combine launched THROUGH tinygrad HCQ (NamedAMDProgram, kernarg captured from a real ggml-hip decode via LD_PRELOAD shim + COV5 hidden-arg block), GPU-busy time (wait=True signal timestamps) vs coop ProfileGraphEvent.
templates.correctness_runner: numpy GQA softmax, rel_max 1.3e-3 (fp16 K/V) — the vendored kernel is the reference; correctness confirms the replay feeds it correctly

gates.structural: n/a (oracle; runs the real llama kernel via HCQ)
gates.local_ab: llama GPU-busy < coop GPU-busy @ctx1024, de-risk target >= 1.5x; MEASURED 2.96x
gates.wd: n/a (never routed; B2 is the W==D step)
gates.promotion_policy: NON-PROMOTABLE — vendored llama reference, not a tinygrad primitive; NEVER a default route

stop_conditions: must never become a default/model route
must never be claimed as a tinygrad primitive win
do not vendor llama machinery beyond the captured kernarg + the on-disk .co
templates.local_ab_result: PASS_ORACLE_LOCAL_AB: vendored llama tile via tinygrad HCQ wins 2.96x GPU-busy @ctx1024 (llama 15.1us vs coop 44.6us), correct (rel 1.3e-3). CONFIRMS the llama-class kernel wins when DISPATCHED BY tinygrad's runtime, not only in llama's own trace. CRITICAL B2 CAVEAT: by WALL time the 2-raw-HCQ-dispatch path is ~2.5x SLOWER (148 vs 58us) -- the GPU win is eaten by per-call launch overhead; a W==D win REQUIRES folding tile+combine into the model JIT graph (one dispatch). 'isolated wins don't transfer to integration' applies.
templates.notes: Route B B1 de-risk (full capture-and-replay). The vendored .co + captured kernarg launch correctly + win on GPU time via HCQ -> the escape hatch is worth integrating AT THE KERNEL LEVEL, but B2 must eliminate per-call launch overhead (graph integration) or the win won't reach W==D. See docs/decode-attention-route-b-b1-result-20260621.md.
